rationale: The cited paper is used in the core theory section, not just in a background list. The citing article explicitly relies on Soman and Gourville (2001) to support the mental-accounting claim that perceived acquisition costs and transaction framing affect postpurchase consumption (e.g., making paid items easier to consume or forgo). This prior result is then integrated into the citing paper's conceptual model as a cost-based pathway, alongside the new salience and convenience mechanisms.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Soman and Gourville (2001) as a conceptual foundation for how perceived acquisition costs/decoupling influence postpurchase consumption decisions. It builds on that foundation by proposing and testing an expanded convenience-salience framework that distinguishes consumption incidence from quantity.
